steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the passage. The passage contrasts two positions: the Times says everyone should do as they like, but culture actively works to move people toward a shared sense of what is beautiful and graceful — getting "the raw person to like that." The main idea is about culture's active role in shaping shared taste.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The process of determining what should be considered beautiful is likely to lead to disagreement.’: the passage describes culture working toward consensus, not predicting disagreement. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘The English are unjustly derided for their sense of taste.’: the passage uses the Times's position only as a foil; it does not defend English taste. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Culture's duty is to use scientific methods to determine what is beautiful.’: scientific methods are never mentioned. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘One of the functions of culture is to create a consensus about matters of good taste.’: this matches the passage's core claim — culture tries to draw people toward shared standards of beauty and gracefulness. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
